agent_summaries.objective: Break N=32 barrier using municipal-level data; test if IDDE→crime relationships hold at finer granularity
Stress-test every key dashboard claim: leave-one-out, winsorize, permutation, alternative specs
Discover nonlinear patterns, feature interactions, and optimal predictors using ML
Test unconventional hypotheses: cifra negra, crime heterogeneity, quantile effects, stationarity, novel combos

top_findings.title: Crime type × IDDE correlation breakdown
top_findings.description: Which specific crime types correlate most/least with IDDE?
top_findings.finding: Most negative (crime ↓ with IDDE): Electorales: r=-0.319; Robo a institución bancaria: r=-0.256; Robo a transportista: r=-0.149. Most positive (crime ↑ with IDDE): Violencia familiar: r=0.576; Abuso sexual: r=0.583; Fraude: r=0.596.
top_findings.recommendation: Crime type heterogeneity reveals which crimes digitalization might address.

top_findings.title: Permutation test: IDDE → municipal crime (N=5000)
top_findings.description: Exact p-value from label-shuffling permutation test, no distributional assumptions
top_findings.finding: Observed r=0.1945. Permutation p=0.0000 (5000 permutations). Significant — this is the most honest p-value possible.
top_findings.recommendation: Permutation test is the gold standard for non-parametric inference. Report this number.

top_findings.title: State IDDE → Municipal crime change (2022→latest)
top_findings.description: Does IDDE predict crime reduction at the municipal level?
top_findings.finding: r=-0.0418, p=0.0373, N=2479. Higher IDDE → crime reduction.
top_findings.recommendation: This is the strongest possible evidence: same municipality, crime change predicted by state IDDE.

top_findings.title: Top predictor → Homicide
top_findings.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
top_findings.finding: Homicide: #1 predictor = graduados_en_programas_stem_xm (importance=0.276). Top 3: graduados_en_programas_st, velocidad_de_descarga_de_, cobertura_de_banda_ancha_
top_findings.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

top_findings.title: Top predictor → Robbery
top_findings.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
top_findings.finding: Robbery: #1 predictor = penetracion_de_banda_ancha_fij (importance=0.225). Top 3: penetracion_de_banda_anch, graduados_en_programas_st, velocidad_de_descarga_de_
top_findings.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

top_findings.title: Top predictor → Fraud
top_findings.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
top_findings.finding: Fraud: #1 predictor = indice_de_desarrollo_digital_e (importance=0.299). Top 3: indice_de_desarrollo_digi, graduados_en_programas_st, penetracion_de_banda_anch
top_findings.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

top_findings.title: Top predictor → Domestic violence
top_findings.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
top_findings.finding: Domestic violence: #1 predictor = empresas_que_utilizan_banca_el (importance=0.324). Top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, cobertura_de_banda_ancha_, graduados_en_programas_st
top_findings.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

top_findings.title: Top predictor → Drug trafficking
top_findings.description: Which digital feature best predicts each crime type?
top_findings.finding: Drug trafficking: #1 predictor = empresas_que_utilizan_banca_el (importance=0.415). Top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, graduados_en_programas_st, cobertura_de_redes_movile
top_findings.recommendation: Different crime types have different predictors. This is a powerful narrative for the dashboard.

top_findings.title: Cifra negra: adjusted vs raw IDDE→crime
top_findings.description: Adjusting crime rates for underreporting using perception as proxy
top_findings.finding: Raw r=0.5984 (p=0.0004). Adjusted r=0.1863 (p=0.3157). Δ=-0.4121. Underreporting inflates the IDDE-crime correlation.
top_findings.recommendation: If delta is large, the dashboard MUST acknowledge cifra negra as a confounder.

top_findings.title: MI vs Pearson: Average wage
top_findings.description: Mutual information (nonlinear) vs Pearson (linear) feature ranking
top_findings.finding: MI top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, penetracion_de_banda_anch, velocidad_de_descarga_de_. Pearson top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, indice_de_desarrollo_digi, penetracion_de_banda_anch. Overlap=2/3. 1 features are nonlinear-only.
top_findings.recommendation: Features that rank high in MI but not in Pearson have nonlinear relationships.

top_findings.title: MI vs Pearson: Crime rate
top_findings.description: Mutual information (nonlinear) vs Pearson (linear) feature ranking
top_findings.finding: MI top 3: cobertura_de_redes_movile, penetracion_de_banda_anch, cobertura_de_banda_ancha_. Pearson top 3: indice_de_desarrollo_digi, penetracion_de_banda_anch, empresas_que_utilizan_ban. Overlap=1/3. 2 features are nonlinear-only.
top_findings.recommendation: Features that rank high in MI but not in Pearson have nonlinear relationships.

top_findings.title: MI vs Pearson: Homicide rate
top_findings.description: Mutual information (nonlinear) vs Pearson (linear) feature ranking
top_findings.finding: MI top 3: graduados_en_programas_st, cobertura_de_redes_movile, cobertura_de_banda_ancha_. Pearson top 3: graduados_en_programas_st, velocidad_de_descarga_de_, penetracion_de_banda_anch. Overlap=1/3. 2 features are nonlinear-only.
top_findings.recommendation: Features that rank high in MI but not in Pearson have nonlinear relationships.

top_findings.title: MI vs Pearson: Fraud rate
top_findings.description: Mutual information (nonlinear) vs Pearson (linear) feature ranking
top_findings.finding: MI top 3: indice_de_desarrollo_digi, graduados_en_programas_st, penetracion_de_banda_anch. Pearson top 3: indice_de_desarrollo_digi, penetracion_de_banda_anch, empresas_que_utilizan_ban. Overlap=2/3. 1 features are nonlinear-only.
top_findings.recommendation: Features that rank high in MI but not in Pearson have nonlinear relationships.

top_findings.title: Threshold effect: IDDE → Wage
top_findings.description: Piecewise linear regression with optimal breakpoint in IDDE
top_findings.finding: Best threshold=130.5, R²_piecewise=0.214, R²_linear=0.163, Δ=+0.050. Threshold effect detected.
top_findings.recommendation: If threshold found, the dashboard can say "above IDDE=X, the relationship changes."

top_findings.title: IDDE → Cyber crime group
top_findings.description: Composite rate of cyber crimes vs IDDE
top_findings.finding: Cyber: r=0.6286, p=0.0002. Positive (crime ↑ with IDDE).
top_findings.recommendation: If Cyber crime shows different direction than total crime, this is a key finding.

top_findings.title: IDDE → Domestic crime group
top_findings.description: Composite rate of domestic crimes vs IDDE
top_findings.finding: Domestic: r=0.5948, p=0.0004. Positive (crime ↑ with IDDE).
top_findings.recommendation: If Domestic crime shows different direction than total crime, this is a key finding.

top_findings.title: IDDE → Large company share (DENUE)
top_findings.description: Does digital development correlate with having more large companies?
top_findings.finding: r(IDDE, large_share)=0.8229, p=0.0000.
top_findings.recommendation: DENUE is completely unused in the dashboard. If significant, adds economic depth.

top_findings.title: Permutation test: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
top_findings.description: Exact p-value from 1000 label-shuffling permutations
top_findings.finding: r=0.7786, permutation p=0.0000 (1000 perms). Confirmed significant.

top_findings.title: Permutation test: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
top_findings.description: Exact p-value from 1000 label-shuffling permutations
top_findings.finding: r=0.6530, permutation p=0.0000 (1000 perms). Confirmed significant.

top_findings.title: Permutation test: IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
top_findings.description: Exact p-value from 1000 label-shuffling permutations
top_findings.finding: r=0.6286, permutation p=0.0000 (1000 perms). Confirmed significant.

top_findings.title: State IDDE → Municipal crime (N=2457)
top_findings.description: State-level IDDE correlated with each municipality crime rate
top_findings.finding: r=0.2561, p=3.75e-38, N=2461. Significant at municipal level.
top_findings.recommendation: If significant, this massively strengthens the dashboard claim with N=2457.

top_findings.title: State IDDE → Violent crime (municipal aggregation)
top_findings.description: Correlation of IDDE with violent crimes aggregated from municipal data
top_findings.finding: Violent crime: r=0.5764, p=0.0006, N=32.
top_findings.recommendation: Violent vs property crime split could reveal which crime types are most affected by digitalization.

top_findings.title: State IDDE → Property crime (municipal aggregation)
top_findings.description: Correlation of IDDE with property crimes aggregated from municipal data
top_findings.finding: Property crime: r=0.5323, p=0.0017, N=32.
top_findings.recommendation: Violent vs property crime split could reveal which crime types are most affected by digitalization.

top_findings.title: Year-by-year municipal IDDE→crime correlation trend
top_findings.description: Is the IDDE-crime relationship stable across years or strengthening/weakening?

top_findings.title: RANSAC robust: IDDE → Wage
top_findings.description: RANSAC identifies outliers automatically and fits to inliers only
top_findings.finding: OLS β=26.4, R²=0.371. RANSAC β=21.3, R²=0.248. Inliers=20, Outliers=11: .

top_findings.title: RANSAC robust: IDDE → Crime
top_findings.description: RANSAC identifies outliers automatically and fits to inliers only
top_findings.finding: OLS β=13.9, R²=0.358. RANSAC β=8.6, R²=-0.015. Inliers=16, Outliers=15: .

top_findings.title: Crime variance decomposition: between vs within states
top_findings.description: ICC (Intraclass Correlation): how much crime variance is between vs within states
top_findings.finding: ICC=1.780. 178.0% of municipal crime variance is BETWEEN states. -78.0% is WITHIN states. State-level analysis captures most variance.
top_findings.recommendation: If ICC < 0.5, the dashboard MUST justify using state-level analysis when most variance is municipal.

top_findings.title: Holm correction: how many correlations survive?
top_findings.description: Sequential Bonferroni (Holm) is stricter than BH. Compares to BH result (105/532).
top_findings.finding: Nominal p<0.05: 166/532. After Holm correction: 20/532. After BH (from EXP-26): 105/532.
top_findings.recommendation: If Holm is much stricter than BH, the dashboard should note which specific correlations are most robust.

top_findings.title: IDDE → Within-state crime heterogeneity
top_findings.description: Does digital development reduce crime variance within states?
top_findings.finding: r(IDDE, CV)=-0.3863, p=0.0290. States with higher IDDE have more uniform crime rates.

top_findings.title: Linear vs RF R²: Average wage
top_findings.description: Does RF capture patterns that linear regression misses?
top_findings.finding: Linear R²=-0.068, RF R²=0.214, Δ=+0.282. Nonlinear patterns present.

promising.title: Interaction effects → Crime rate

promising.finding: Linear R²=-0.864, With interactions R²=-0.689, Δ=+0.175. N_interactions=6.

promising.title: Dose-response quartiles: IDDE → Wage
promising.description: Mean of target by IDDE quartile to detect nonlinearity

promising.title: Per-cluster LOOCV → Wage
promising.description: Does the model predict differently within each digitalization cluster?

promising.title: Per-cluster LOOCV → Crime
promising.description: Does the model predict differently within each digitalization cluster?

promising.title: PDP monotonicity: IDDE → Wage
promising.description: Is the partial dependence curve monotonic or does it have reversals?
promising.finding: PDP range: [9509.2, 13325.2]. Monotonic=False. 15 increasing steps, 3 decreasing steps.
promising.recommendation: Non-monotonic PDP means "more IDDE is not always better" — powerful nuance.

promising.title: PDP monotonicity: IDDE → Crime
promising.description: Is the partial dependence curve monotonic or does it have reversals?
promising.finding: PDP range: [465.9, 2497.3]. Monotonic=False. 14 increasing steps, 4 decreasing steps.
promising.recommendation: Non-monotonic PDP means "more IDDE is not always better" — powerful nuance.

promising.title: Composite score → Crime
promising.description: Average of 3 standardized variables (IDDE + employment + wages)

promising.title: Composite score → Family trust
promising.description: Average of 3 standardized variables (IDDE + employment + wages)

promising.title: IDDE → Male victim share
promising.description: Does digitalization correlate with the gender distribution of crime victims?
promising.finding: r(IDDE, male_share)=-0.2253, p=0.2231.

promising.title: Leave South out: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
promising.description: Correlation after removing all South states
promising.finding: Full r=0.7786, without South r=0.6565, Δ=-0.1221.

promising.title: Leave South out: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
promising.description: Correlation after removing all South states
promising.finding: Full r=0.6530, without South r=0.5650, Δ=-0.0880.

promising.title: RFE top 3 features → Average wage
promising.description: Recursive Feature Elimination: which 3 features matter most?
promising.finding: Selected: empresas_que_utilizan_ban, velocidad_de_descarga_de_, indice_de_desarrollo_digi. R²(3 features)=0.180, R²(all)=0.172.

promising.title: RFE top 3 features → Crime rate
promising.description: Recursive Feature Elimination: which 3 features matter most?
promising.finding: Selected: cobertura_de_banda_ancha_, penetracion_de_banda_anch, graduados_en_programas_st. R²(3 features)=-0.511, R²(all)=-0.655.

promising.title: RFE top 3 features → Homicide rate
promising.description: Recursive Feature Elimination: which 3 features matter most?
promising.finding: Selected: cobertura_de_banda_ancha_, graduados_en_programas_st, velocidad_de_descarga_de_. R²(3 features)=-0.384, R²(all)=-0.603.

promising.title: Population-weighted IDDE → municipal crime
promising.description: Correlation weighted by municipality population (big cities count more)
promising.finding: Weighted r=0.2004 vs unweighted r from 1.1. Unweighted correlation is stronger.

promising.title: Spearman vs Pearson: IDDE → Safety perception (R²=0.45)
promising.description: Does rank correlation differ from Pearson? Tests for nonlinearity/outliers.
promising.finding: Pearson r=0.2493, Spearman ρ=0.3738, Δ=+0.1245. Nonlinearity detected.

promising.title: Panel year-by-year: IDDE → avg_wage
promising.description: Does the cross-sectional relationship hold in each year separately?

promising.title: Panel year-by-year: IDDE → crime_rate_100k
promising.description: Does the cross-sectional relationship hold in each year separately?

promising.title: Stacking → Average wage
promising.description: Stacked ensemble: Linear + RF + GB → meta-learner (LOO)
promising.finding: Stacking R²=0.513, best base R²=0.478, Δ=+0.035. Weights: LR=-0.32, RF=0.81, GB=0.44

promising.title: Stacking → Crime rate
promising.description: Stacked ensemble: Linear + RF + GB → meta-learner (LOO)
promising.finding: Stacking R²=0.284, best base R²=0.165, Δ=+0.119. Weights: LR=0.41, RF=-0.35, GB=0.66

promising.title: Stacking → Homicide rate
promising.description: Stacked ensemble: Linear + RF + GB → meta-learner (LOO)
promising.finding: Stacking R²=0.209, best base R²=-0.054, Δ=+0.263. Weights: LR=0.54, RF=-0.94, GB=0.12

promising.title: R² stability (70% subsamples): IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
promising.description: Distribution of R² across 500 random 70% subsets
promising.finding: R² median=0.4327, IQR=0.1184. Unstable — sensitive to sample composition.

promising.title: R² stability (70% subsamples): IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
promising.description: Distribution of R² across 500 random 70% subsets
promising.finding: R² median=0.4076, IQR=0.1269. Unstable — sensitive to sample composition.

promising.title: High vs low IDDE states: municipal crime distribution
promising.description: Non-parametric test of whether municipal crime rates differ by state IDDE level
promising.finding: Mann-Whitney U=767616, p=0.0000. Cohen d=0.265. High IDDE mean=41.1, Low IDDE mean=11.1.

promising.title: State IDDE → Crime in Interior municipalities
promising.description: Border states have different crime dynamics (narco-trafficking)

promising.title: Entropy-based: IDDE ↔ Crime
promising.description: Normalized mutual information from discretized variables (captures nonlinear deps)
promising.finding: MI=0.432, NMI=0.216 (normalized to [0,1]). Compare to Pearson r² from linear correlation.

promising.title: Entropy-based: IDDE ↔ Wage
promising.description: Normalized mutual information from discretized variables (captures nonlinear deps)
promising.finding: MI=0.484, NMI=0.242 (normalized to [0,1]). Compare to Pearson r² from linear correlation.

promising.title: Natural experiment: top vs bottom IDDE changers
promising.description: Top 5 IDDE changers (Aguascal, Coahuila, Tlaxcala, Hidalgo, Ciudad d) vs bottom 5
promising.finding: Top 5 crime Δ: -22.9. Bottom 5 crime Δ: -373.9. Diff: 351.0. Top IDDE changers did NOT reduce crime more.

promising.title: LOO influence: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
promising.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
promising.finding: Full r=0.7786. Max Δr=0.0283 (removing San Luis Potosí). Robust.
promising.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by San Luis Potosí. Must disclose.

promising.title: LOO influence: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
promising.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
promising.finding: Full r=0.6530. Max Δr=0.0620 (removing Oaxaca). Robust.
promising.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by Oaxaca. Must disclose.

promising.title: LOO influence: IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
promising.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
promising.finding: Full r=0.6286. Max Δr=0.0757 (removing Ciudad de México). Robust.
promising.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by Ciudad de México. Must disclose.

promising.title: LOO influence: E-banking → Wage (R²=0.43)
promising.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
promising.finding: Full r=0.6525. Max Δr=0.0452 (removing Querétaro). Robust.
promising.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by Querétaro. Must disclose.

promising.title: LOO influence: IDDE → Safety perception (R²=0.45)
promising.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
promising.finding: Full r=0.2493. Max Δr=0.0857 (removing Ciudad de México). Robust.
promising.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by Ciudad de México. Must disclose.

promising.title: LOO influence: BB coverage → Wage (r=0.67)
promising.description: How much does removing one state change the correlation?
promising.finding: Full r=0.4750. Max Δr=0.0726 (removing San Luis Potosí). Robust.
promising.recommendation: If max_delta > 0.10, the correlation is driven by San Luis Potosí. Must disclose.

promising.title: Bootstrap 80% stability: BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
promising.description: Correlation stability when using 80% subsamples (500 iterations)
promising.finding: Bootstrap r: median=0.7804, 95% CI=[0.6976, 0.8372]. Range width=0.1396.

promising.title: Bootstrap 80% stability: IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
promising.description: Correlation stability when using 80% subsamples (500 iterations)
promising.finding: Bootstrap r: median=0.6536, 95% CI=[0.5398, 0.7542]. Range width=0.2144.

promising.title: Bootstrap 80% stability: IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
promising.description: Correlation stability when using 80% subsamples (500 iterations)
promising.finding: Bootstrap r: median=0.6331, 95% CI=[0.4793, 0.7361]. Range width=0.2568.

promising.title: State IDDE → Crime in Rural municipalities
promising.description: Correlation of state IDDE with crime in rural municipalities

promising.title: State IDDE → Crime in Urban municipalities
promising.description: Correlation of state IDDE with crime in urban municipalities

promising.description: Do large or small municipalities show stronger IDDE-crime correlation?

promising.description: Do large or small municipalities show stronger IDDE-crime correlation?

promising.description: Which features survive regularization longest?
promising.finding: Top 3 (last to be zeroed): cobertura_de_banda_ancha_(α=2099.2559), penetracion_de_banda_anch(α=2099.2559), cobertura_de_redes_movile(α=2099.2559)

promising.title: Ridge+Poly → Average wage
promising.description: Ridge regression with degree-2 polynomial features
promising.finding: Linear R²=-0.068, Ridge+Poly R²=-0.179, Δ=-0.110.

promising.title: Ridge+Poly → Crime rate
promising.description: Ridge regression with degree-2 polynomial features
promising.finding: Linear R²=-0.492, Ridge+Poly R²=-0.243, Δ=+0.249.

promising.title: Ridge+Poly → Homicide rate
promising.description: Ridge regression with degree-2 polynomial features
promising.finding: Linear R²=-0.358, Ridge+Poly R²=-0.127, Δ=+0.231.

promising.title: Rank-rank: IDDE → Wage
promising.description: Rank correlation removes scale effects; tests pure ordinal relationship

promising.title: Rank-rank: IDDE → Crime
promising.description: Rank correlation removes scale effects; tests pure ordinal relationship

promising.title: Quantile regression: IDDE → Wage
promising.description: Does the IDDE-wage relationship differ at the 25th vs 75th percentile?
promising.finding: Q25: β=15.7; Q50: β=26.4; Q75: β=38.5. Spread=22.8. Effect is similar across quantiles.
promising.recommendation: If spread is large, IDDE benefits high-wage states more (or less).

promising.title: Spearman rank correlation: IDDE → municipal crime
promising.description: Non-parametric rank correlation, robust to outliers and nonlinear monotonic relationships
promising.finding: ρ=0.4390, p=0.0000, N=2486. Compare to Pearson r from 1.1 to assess outlier influence.

promising.title: Winsorized (5%): BB coverage → Friend trust (r=0.78)
promising.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
promising.finding: Winsorized r=0.7945 vs original r=0.7786. Δ=0.0159. Robust to outliers.

promising.title: Winsorized (5%): IDDE → Family trust (r=0.65)
promising.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
promising.finding: Winsorized r=0.6719 vs original r=0.6530. Δ=0.0190. Robust to outliers.

promising.title: Winsorized (5%): IDDE → Fraud (r=0.63)
promising.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
promising.finding: Winsorized r=0.6028 vs original r=0.6286. Δ=0.0258. Robust to outliers.

promising.title: Winsorized (5%): E-banking → Wage (R²=0.43)
promising.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
promising.finding: Winsorized r=0.6509 vs original r=0.6525. Δ=0.0016. Robust to outliers.

promising.title: Winsorized (5%): IDDE → Safety perception (R²=0.45)
promising.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
promising.finding: Winsorized r=0.3019 vs original r=0.2493. Δ=0.0526. Sensitive to outliers.

promising.title: Winsorized (5%): BB coverage → Wage (r=0.67)
promising.description: Correlation after clipping extreme values at 2.5%/97.5%
promising.finding: Winsorized r=0.4637 vs original r=0.4750. Δ=0.0113. Robust to outliers.

promising.title: IDDE panel stationarity check
promising.description: Is IDDE trending? If non-stationary, panel regression results may be spurious.
promising.finding: Trend slope=0.897/year, R²=0.442, p=0.3348. Var(diff)/Var(level)=1.691. No clear trend. With only 4 years, formal ADF is unreliable.
promising.recommendation: If IDDE is trending, panel FE might capture spurious trends. Acknowledge in defense doc.

promising.title: RF permutation importance → Average wage
promising.description: Random Forest with N=31, 200 trees, max_depth=3
promising.finding: R²_train=0.883, R²_LOOCV=nan. Top 3: empresas_que_utilizan_banca_el=1.1522±0.2759, penetracion_de_banda_ancha_fij=0.0756±0.0232, velocidad_de_descarga_de_banda=0.0718±0.0169
promising.recommendation: If LOOCV R² > 0.2, there are genuine nonlinear patterns. Report feature importances.
